question: If a software intelligence explosion compresses 3+ years of AI progress into <1 year with ~60% probability, what does Davidson, Hadshar, and MacAskill's categorisation of IE types suggest about whether this compressed scenario is most likely a software, AI-technology, or full-stack IE - and what are the implications for which actors can control such an explosion?

expected_citations.title: How quick and big would a software intelligence explosion be?

required_elements: The 'Three Types' paper identifies software IE as having the shortest feedback-loop lags (~3 months training cycles)
The 'Quick & Big' paper assigns ~60% probability to software IE compressing 3+ years into <1 year
Software IE concentrates power within one country or company because it requires no chip-fabrication scale-up
Full-stack IE would necessarily spread across multiple countries and industries (chip fabs are geographically distributed)
Integration: combining the timeline (~60% for fast software IE) with the type categorisation, fast compression scenarios are systematically more likely to favor concentrated control
